name: Two Hard-Boiled Eggs
popularityContext: A simple, traditional protein snack recommended by British Lion Eggs and widely eaten in the UK as a portable, no-fuss high-protein option; commonly batch-boiled at the start of the week for grab-and-go snacking.
prepNotes: Place eggs in a pan of cold water, bring to the boil, then simmer for 8-10 minutes for a firm yolk. Cool under cold running water, peel, and store in the fridge for up to a week. Eat plain or with a pinch of salt.

nutrition.method: Calculated from ingredient composition via McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods

prepNotes: Split the scone in half. Spread each half with jam, then top with a generous spoonful of clotted cream (Cornish tradition: jam first; Devon tradition: cream first). Serve with a pot of tea.

name: Marmite on Toast
popularityContext: Marmite on toast is a distinctively British savoury snack built around the famous yeast-extract spread, eaten as a quick snack or light breakfast in households nationwide, often described as a love-it-or-hate-it national icon.
prepNotes: Toast the bread until golden. Spread with a thin layer of butter, then add a thin layer of Marmite on top (a little goes a long way given its strong, salty flavour). Serve warm.

nutrition.method: Calculated from ingredient composition via McCance and Widdowson's Composition of Foods, cross-checked against Marmite UK nutrition information

regionAvailability.notes: Marmite, wholemeal bread and butter are sold in every UK supermarket nationwide; one of the most budget-friendly snacks available.

name: Yop Strawberry Drinking Yoghurt
popularityContext: Yop is a long-running Yoplait brand and one of the UK's best-known drinking yoghurts, a familiar chilled-aisle item especially popular with children and as a quick on-the-go snack.
prepNotes: Shake the bottle and drink chilled, straight from the bottle. No preparation needed.

nutrition.method: Calculated from Yoplait UK product nutrition panel

regionAvailability.notes: Sold in single 200g bottles and multipacks in the chilled dairy aisle of every major UK supermarket; a common kids' lunchbox and fridge staple.

name: Tesco Fruit and Nut Mix with Chocolate (Snack Pack)
popularityContext: Pre-portioned fruit, nut and chocolate trail mix packs are widely sold in UK supermarkets as a balanced grab-and-go snack, positioned between a purely indulgent treat and a purely 'healthy' nut pack.

ingredients.item: Tesco fruit and nut mix with chocolate

prepNotes: Open the pack and eat directly; no preparation required.

nutrition.method: Calculated from Tesco UK product nutrition panel

regionAvailability.notes: Sold as single snack-pack portions and larger sharing bags in the world foods/healthy snacking aisle of major UK supermarkets.

name: Tesco Cheese & Pickle Sandwich (Meal Deal Snack Portion)
popularityContext: Cheese and pickle (Branston-style) is one of the UK's classic sandwich fillings and a top-selling meal-deal sandwich at Tesco, Boots and other UK food-to-go counters; commonly bought as a between-meals snack rather than a full lunch.
prepNotes: Bought ready to eat from a supermarket meal-deal chiller. No preparation required; eaten cold straight from the pack.

nutrition.method: Calculated from Tesco UK published product nutrition panel

regionAvailability.notes: Cheese and pickle sandwiches are sold pre-packaged in every UK supermarket meal-deal chiller and high-street food-to-go chain nationwide; also simple and cheaper to make at home.
